textByHashes.fc02869eaf70ada801300af867c8ab7a57e80526: Ellamaria? Well, if you're in her service my guidance is this: do the fastest run that you can off the shortest pier you can find.
textByHashes.31323ecaa28d01da8a5259bbdc227acca49b6de8: Oh yes, we go way back, long before she married that fool the King of Varrock. She wasn't always royal you know.
textByHashes.ba8e30d873674adfa8941f8ff9facbec2f79dadc: No, in her youth she was a barmaid at the Blue Moon inn. All that fancy talk, that's just for show. No, the Ellamaria I knew was as common as muck, until she lucked onto good King Roald.
textByHashes.54f23f5fac6fba8529744ae8b215e41fed9ad830: So what sort of guidance has Little-Miss-Hoity-Toity sent you to seek?
textByHashes.c3abbfbe38b50e45999e4d565425efcaad9f5f1e: That is an area that - luckily for you, young - I have studied. Have you heard of the diplomat known as Charos?
textByHashes.50c1c3d1147b2ef5940bf4ba52149ccddec4e188: You have found the Ring of Charos?!? Tell me - do you still have it in your possession?
textByHashes.2ed98f3f075ad64625c8255af061d6aa092a5599: Important?!? This ring possesses hidden power beyond belief! Go and get it, and I will tell you its secrets.
textByHashes.7b8a1c772a256ba8dfbe018093e72077e697bc3a: Lost the Ring of Charos?!? That is indeed a disastrous turn of events. This ring that you have so casually thrown away possesses hidden power beyond belief.
textByHashes.23d69477fd97ce541710bb5688c66fe9572d2d7c: If everything I have heard about Charos's ring is true, it will not stay lost forever. Where did you originally find it?
textByHashes.e5d1552e5ef727f546672ccabaac99e5eaa71135: In order to find the ring it may be wise to return to Fenkenstrain - he may have found a way to regain it. If you manage to reobtain the ring, return to me and I will reveal its secrets to you.
textByHashes.b226fff80ca9d072eb6bc41084c699063bf4f282: Charos rose to power under the rule of Lord Drakan of Morytania, his only living servant. The Vampyre Lord suffered the existence of Charos because of the talents that this sophisticated diplomat had to offer.
textByHashes.fde49fd33fc4270d52b85835012a87ddd230b773: Indeed. Charos had many uses - spy, assassin, negotiator - because he could convince anyone to do his wishes. These talents were in some part Charos's own, but greatly enhanced by the ring that he fashioned.
textByHashes.295ffda6729c5f4d49297809e968b7d13983238f: Exactly. One who wears the ring must possess some innate ability of their own, which the ring enhances so that the wearer can convince all but the most strong- minded to do their bidding.
textByHashes.5331b10fa916037c19752e12d912ad7bfc272034: Charos had a castle built on the northern coast of Morytania, where he dwelt for many years in service to Drakan. None really know what became of him, but some say that he became too certain of his abilities,
textByHashes.dca7d9f8450301ff9dfbfa0147ff5df836639c64: and turned them on his master. This would have been his ultimate downfall, for the ring's power would have had little or no effect upon Drakan.
textByHashes.f88006f5e8afd1c4d88930d8631be135c31ba500: Fenkenstrain probably found the ring many years later, buried somewhere within the depths of Charos's castle.
textByHashes.83013691ad03db8d55e8fe0e7a8129b311cdff22: ...he would naturally come looking for the ring that is his by right. But I don't believe that you should worry about that - it has been many years since his disappearance, and the likelyhood is that he is dead.
textByHashes.34d2cc94cfe81542a65d9e516597ed4b7a8c6fe5: I could activate the power within this ring for you, but for me to do that I must know that you will use it wisely. To do that I must ask you a few questions, to see how good a diplomat you can be.
textByHashes.653cc003e6754246c553b01a7f4a1bfe2fcd569d: ... but I'm afraid that you don't seem to have the aptitude. To you, the Ring of Charos is but a worthless trinket.
textByHashes.6eb6c7b7e304ae3e50d8c6d3d6dc9e888ed6af15: Well, if you think it will do any good ... here goes then-
textByHashes.6558c7717db79ed75a48d8ecbcbb78dca038725d: Now look, don't start making me feel sorry for you...
textByHashes.7a8d3189df25f23a761057df416268e7959a7025: I didn't say that - look, if you want another try, come back and speak to me again.
textByHashes.899d1d7f825d0cf8da1821dc4f6f807ce5fde823: ... Congratulations! You have clearly got what it takes to use the Ring of Charos. Wait a moment, ...
textByHashes.2401bbf3281d836e1eb2b0ccba5bd251d8a84954: The Ring of Charos has now been opened to you, . You may use its power to charm the weak-minded, but it will only work whilst you are wearing it.
textByHashes.a09bea38e5184a55c618889538cc2bc162666f32: Ha ha! I thought you might try that!! But remember, - the power of the Ring of Charos will not work with everybody, especially not a wise old bird like me!
textByHashes.c8c6907d937a0a8c1c2270913a2c0c527152e2e8: Funny, I thought you would take the chance to test the power of the ring on me! No matter - leave me be now, I have important surveillance duties to carry out.
textByHashes.d28b79c886a5524f7560792e7d52c1a07d5c1197: I don't see it on you - do you have it in your bag?
textByHashes.bb20ee25562dbade884a4ccfef392bc04413932f: Well go and get it then, and I will tell you its secrets.
textByHashes.e7d2b904742353e0b64e80b19c573028e7929341: The Ring of Charos? What's that?
textByHashes.5ed724048cb4fed5019562be39c2e4ff09e82ab1: Just joking, - good good, I will now tell you what this ring is capable of.
textByHashes.a414a0c28f0676a9c88533e4dcfcd2ae0062c60e: Do you have the ring with you?
textByHashes.d701b0ab8d83b27eb9ab9cd66573abe007b1de04: Yes, yes get it and return back to me.
textByHashes.eaf240d9fbb5f2efcde13529eeb39e7b8be32f16: It's good of you to pop in, . What brings you to Draynor today?
textByHashes.95364c8f561dc97c4193a4db536b9044a24a6b21: You should see a doctor about your arm.
textByHashes.dd61a72eb3aa139aecc83bb36bb80bed6887d41c: ... you should see a doctor about your brain.
textByHashes.a3beaa2dddcac9a64f070ebcefd29b01700789a7: You asked me to pretend I'm dead. That's what I imagine it would be like.
textByHashes.91587b09882d7d8bc2827faaff7788db69b1ed81: I remember driving trolls out of those tunnels, yes. So now there's a troll called My Arm who wants to pretend he's killed me in revenge, to impress his girlfriend's father, who is called Mother?
textByHashes.52216383e0fd10f61213084777397b6b77461b61: Hmmmph. That's quite a big favour you're asking. I've spent much of my life saving people from monsters like that, not providing family mediation services for them.
textByHashes.f7a9b94aebdf25450b3e95316941bffa079b4620: I don't think so, thank you. In my experience, a troll's idea of a quest reward is probably something useless like a pile of burnt meat.
textByHashes.8597cfe5a4645f43e22ffc874a513028736434cf: I suppose you did save my life from the Sea Troll Queen. That has to count for something...
textByHashes.d29c2d193787c1569e8c84ae5ff050790068443c: Very well, I'll help. I'll need a cadava potion from the Varrock Apothecary, calibrated to suit my age. Also build me a proper mahogany coffin here - I wouldn't be seen dead in a cheap oak or teak one.
textByHashes.93ae14851926e734e27c299296f50afb08258fa7: I asked you to get me a cadava potion from the Varrock Apothecary, calibrated to suit my age, and to build me a mahogany coffin. How are you getting on?
textByHashes.8dbffaa88326409e275485e88b7445a5b1330bc7: So I see. Make sure it's mahogany when you do - I wouldn't be seen dead in a cheap oak or teak one. You can build it here.
textByHashes.98990dba2c8e3b9ecdf71797b319a4e258d248f1: Really? He's in Varrock, near the south-west corner of the city. He's a funny chap, but he knows his trade.
textByHashes.1e8e40ad5bb2df0943fc6f4aec3b755f1cab2e45: Maybe I should have told you to make a gilded one, with a marble pedestal and crystal ornamentation. It would look more fitting... but no, this will do.
textByHashes.da63e891b40d11e8091ea652b695d6518f645eb8: Good. Hand it to me, then let us proceed with this charade.
textByHashes.5c29ba3d74181b5c954f131594fb94314a808bbf: Yes, that looks about right. I wouldn't want to drink more than this, or I might risk an overdose. Well, here goes...
textByHashes.fee1e1c5791ad20d4f59832020ce0c58a2c88ff5: Those salt formations are really quite remarkable. Your troll friends are having a private moment, so I'll study these while my head recovers from that potion.
textByHashes.adabc276618a5231d0dd0864c306089dca482ee0: I don't think I could get in there either, even if the smell weren't abhorrent. But might be able to fit.
textByHashes.6fdaa57969b94d02d65399b9e25f33b367b14d90: Anyway, Odd Mushroom was telling us about the fires he'd invented.
textByHashes.33b05ef20480f270d22f043fdf7b3de077891dcb: I hate to be indelicate, but... it may not be possible to overthrow Mother without killing him, especially if the fire makes him as dangerous as Odd Mushroom said.
textByHashes.042ec49c20d685044e2aca759bfedff33ba65175: , you heard Odd Mushroom's warning. We can't use our teleport magicks, so the only escape - for you - is down the smelly hole.
textByHashes.f4ce4f2e473e77a4d9acbbec2743e64eecb020a2: Also, if we drop anything in here, I bet you're not going to be able to retrieve it. Items dropped in here will be lost forever.
textByHashes.5da679ed6387b9c48f9324442038b8f9ed1a9d27: I'm not sure this was my proudest adventure, but you're welcome. I think I'll head off home to Draynor quite soon.
textByHashes.917041683c8ae09a2c9813d472298da69ab4502a: Let's not emphasise that bit now, eh?
textByHashes.e4e981262245712fada85a9c65e51ce5f024d80d: Well, that was jolly charming, but I'm off now. Good bye.
textByHashes.94f9c46dd15378fecb44f7348491975eb832a963: Less of the 'old' man, if you please!
textByHashes.13ee1d9067fed1f71f99eda1a56b05cec3d1377a: But yes, I suppose you could say that. I prefer to think of myself as a sage.
textByHashes.f5f1990f90d666abc5f15d5932e0d6a7e3e9af43: I've spent most of my life studying this world in which we live. I've strode through the depths of the deadliest dungeons, roamed the murky jungles of Karamja, meditated on the glories of Saradomin on Entrana,
textByHashes.009509aa8a9de1ddda458da8c250fbae8ade9f01: and read dusty tomes in the Library of Varrock.
textByHashes.e48d2aa1b6b5aa4ba84f5efdf978a5f93f899930: Now I'm not as young as I used to be, I'm settling here where it's peaceful.
textByHashes.1d560e17897cdf2eb07a3158eb2c82645fda74ee: It's a pity about that vampyre that keeps attacking the village. At least Saradomin protects me.
textByHashes.2e0c62afe3a3d5831dee54ed204918ab40206514: Now I'm here, perhaps I could offer you the benefit of my experience and wisdom?
textByHashes.54085490b4cd4942815a3faf38a7347d54721dcd: Well, I imagine you've gathered up quite a lot of stuff on your travels. Things you used for quests a long time ago that you don't need now.
textByHashes.42dab890a96f9ebc74aa0cac13f7fe83be1b6000: If you like, I can look through your bank and see if there's anything you can chuck away.
textByHashes.8cc1e5d2c24d42e2754cbfc161ae0655a7ffe673: Alternatively, you can bring items here and show them to me. If I see that it's something you don't need, I'll let you know. I might even be willing to buy it.
textByHashes.5c999c38f20570bb138dcdb16f365f3bbff39820: Yes, that's right. Or I'd be happy to chat with you about the wonders of this world!
textByHashes.22e687f55ffc3a92e3bcceb8cfc500a99bb09d56: The Evil Chicken! Many are the myths and legends surrounding this foul bird. Some say that it is the result of a mad mage's attempt to summon a demon, others say that it was once a
textByHashes.d14edf94b133b4412b81dfda278c17a6e77a1877: normal chicken that realised how limited its farmyard life was. It threw off its chains and attempted to unite the other chickens in revenge against their human captors.
textByHashes.7c4f6cea50138434e97e61da56447d7958345237: When that failed the Evil Chicken grew angry with its brethren and swore to kill any human or chicken that it ever encountered again!
textByHashes.aa92862a6122bef0a7b0f006d461082b3d05e7e4: It is whispered that there is a coven of witches that meet, on the night of the full moon in the fabled fairy kingdom, to worship this bird and that they can summon it to do their twisted will!
textByHashes.b7d58144cd7904a1e35fb1372f367dc392aa371b: I would start in the lands of the fairies, possibly there is some truth in the rumours of summoning the chicken there. Actually, I've done further research, it may be around
textByHashes.2473a8253190e03391fee09f9c37507ea50702b8: here somewhere, if you're really thinking of going there you should be extremely careful, it's claimed that the chicken has forced some very powerful creatures to guard his lair.
textByHashes.1e9d9e0fd11264220ac334c71e803b8787a85042: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?
textByHashes.f7296052ec4bebe7dc25dddfaaa94ce8114c3300: Lie still, and trouble us no more.
textByHashes.b943796c1d0ecaee4f1da61b695d8962294f95a2: Heh heh! Yes, I suppose you could say that. But that was all quite a long time ago. I haven't been on what you'd call a 'quest' for a while.
textByHashes.6c6deb53b5e4e45e97a8f71fc7f8ac1786f4e9b6: Oh? What exactly had you in mind?
textByHashes.9f30562717c9ef1dc91d5b4ffe3a0611c31cfe3a: Hmmm... so I haven't been forgotten... and the world once again is calling for my aid...
textByHashes.9187bd4a7cfc8889fce92be28a93465507b54d4a: ... yet this could be the opportunity I've been waiting for...
textByHashes.daa718681df7b915072b6486e6173affa7de3253: ... but I'm a lot older than I used to be, do I still have the strength? Should I retire now, or can I do just one more quest?
textByHashes.4c416f33975738cdebfc4501540cbdaf78ffae58: Yes, I'll do it! This can be my final adventure, my last great quest. Saving this colony shall be my swan song.
textByHashes.b1c73198c7613d5db74e515753c460bd407be036: Legend tells us that a swan, before it dies, sings a song of outstanding beauty as a fitting conclusion to its graceful life. This adventure will be my swan song.
textByHashes.0121bd734c2920da60168922ed3ddb1394d16b8a: No, you ignoramus! I shall bring my adventuring days to a dramatic close by saving this colony!
textByHashes.190c239ee349bd49f1e893e78bc02938efb41064: Yes, that's right, I'm not going to sing. Anyway, I shall need some supplies from you.
textByHashes.2ac000e102bfa84d9ae7b91f1d7963afb96685f8: I'm rather short of runes. If you could fetch me 5 Blood runes, 10 Lava runes and 10 Mist runes, I'll be able to set off.
textByHashes.8ae62e4ad34fe84f9bbae11819b66aad0006dc0f: Excellent. I'll see you outside the Colony. Don't forget to arm yourself - it could be dangerous!
textByHashes.5d23165290ef56659c82c6caaa650aef687e4db1: I'm guessing you haven't got them all, have you?
textByHashes.35b283b2b42cd148b8b79f5b6b6fd31829d2072e: Well, do get a move on! I need 5 Blood runes, 10 Lava runes and 10 Mist runes, otherwise I'll be no use in a fight.
textByHashes.48e828abfb74d0cd2b730351460b53ef909c72e7: Hello again, ! I must thank you for persuading me to come - I feel quite invigorated in the sea breeze!
textByHashes.5b3b1c7872d8a05e0b40c292d9391f799773726c: Are you sure you're ready? I can hear creatures moving around in the trees, and we might have to do some fighting soon.
textByHashes.3f2315693a59db549f8d2cb46fa47c8a5e7767ed: Excellent. I think this little tunnel will lead us there...
textByHashes.9d9d25b6ad37166cb547ed256b5e87542b4371bf: Yes, better safe than sorry! We can go through this little tunnel when you're ready.
textByHashes.55ea3b2a34d1f9d2451f68c65d8201ed43a61817: Just hop into the tunnel when you're ready.
textByHashes.e84f793ca15478e2c0f92a7fabb3f7aa2ed8cb07: Well, it all seems to have gone smoothly so far!
textByHashes.94aeae04300a628e220cbdd02dfea8af458a5add: Please go and tell Herman I'm here. Someone's sure to know where to find him.
textByHashes.3a833c4b24a54e198c2bf10008b7a2c01dd1b30e: Yes, that's right. The sheer number of trolls means that the best hope for the Colony is to gather our forces and wipe out all the trolls at once.
textByHashes.0e18faa29c1ec7cceef78c47589fd729c71b61ae: Yes, with the bit about gathering our forces.
textByHashes.3a6518a21c38c6f7557774ef89ce78b7859a2975: So what you need to do, , is to fetch us an army!
textByHashes.35e54e0c95f7c8c634f004493e71dec5795a3f55: In the Wizards' Guild in Yanille, Wizard Frumscone always seems to have a limitless supply of zombies and similar creatures. If you can persuade him to lend us an army, we should be able to defeat these trolls.
textByHashes.b4d4b29a0132ff2b4f71dfd29eef16ad82f359bf: Hmmmph. I don't much like the sound of this. I certainly won't be sending anyone to Frumscone for help again!
textByHashes.5b87ab970573f83d74b66e66c3dc37360edde918: Well, I suppose so. , this could be VERY dangerous. Do you feel ready to fight?
textByHashes.6ab32d0f14ed5ad4d902ed48d28a719945921cf3: Yes, of course. But you, my trusty sidekick, are in this adventure with me, and if I'm going forth into battle, you should make sure you're ready too.
textByHashes.77071a5606a62f24dae9a646f08ccd2f1481c92e: Good. May this, my final adventure, end well!
textByHashes.1e4def096b31426418558e309a90199bd0012b9e: I... can't... move... , you must fight this for me...
textByHashes.2ee82fff33e34fee2f769b3aebd85503a32e27fa: Your fight was truly magnificent, ! Congratulations!
textByHashes.6fc16f02c99340b26569bbdc1abb011b7ab4a866: I think you've earned a little something from me, too.
textByHashes.608d58d4abe3ace71a3837af72125c7aeb9efd87: 'Old man'... yes, I am old. That creature nearly killed me - I have never been so close to defeat.
textByHashes.a5d10d144d713320bfa151560e3a861295239db0: Yes, we won... YOU won... It is definitely time I retired.
textByHashes.43b84f2b6d1712e135f2bcb1ce7fae156b2bd9ab: Well, I'm sure I'll find some way to keep myself busy. But yes, this has been my last quest. My swan song.
textByHashes.5643d85b23a0f88774045f38fa34dc7ac266d8c5: It's been a pleasure working with you too. Do pop in to see me in Draynor soon! I leave you with my blessing...
textByHashes.dbd0d706cb8a1dd7fdd50e814611402c23a616b3: It's Dionysius here. I'm using my magic to communicate to you. Don't worry.
textByHashes.f47faf34381eb45ad7c29929691cfc64a3d06189: I just wanted to say congratulations . I've been watching you in your adventures - very well done.
textByHashes.1369d63e2b6d48a02d2a410ce645b0137c6e626f: and read some dusty tomes in the Library of Varrock.
textByHashes.6de0ae32ef57d8e9eda9946dfd13471107e4b05f: I'm not giving out free money, but if you try again later I'd be glad to reward you if you'd do a little job for me.
textByHashes.9b716f0e367414400daecf932c00f56387577dac: I'm not giving out free money, but I'd be happy to reward you if you'll do a little job for me.
textByHashes.eabcbc93a4f382258afff5ca844a25cc3b71b2d1: I'm sure I can think of a few little jobs. This won't be a quest, mind you, just a little favour...
textByHashes.16641fd18f2c32f393a68a823ed5ec0771b0279b: You don't appear to have enough space in your inventory. Come back and see me when you do.
textByHashes.bfcce7c54f2a5e137d7b891450210e86918c29cb: All skillcapes come with a hood - you're not going to be able to hold them now. Try again another time.
textByHashes.f6fd824c9a580fc55616c9b20084682c6ca89fdd: Thanks, but I don't have anything I need doing right now.
textByHashes.2e7cca328663e15db54a301176e89efc60c2dc8c: If Entrana is a land dedicated to the glory of Saradomin, the Wilderness is surely the land of Zamorak.
textByHashes.ce6245ec3ebbc6d7f67a57fac542c43b031c79cd: It's a dangerous place, where adventurers such as yourself may attack each other, using all their combat skills in the struggle for survival.
textByHashes.d91aedddfd99108b7f68d9cfa3c13592b7b947fc: The Wilderness has different levels. In a low level area, you can only fight adventurers whose combat level is close to yours.
textByHashes.fb3b0b6c2c93bec77bc20d53618b94fb1d92f658: But if you venture into the high level areas in the far north, you can be attacked by adventurers who are significantly stronger than you.
textByHashes.f349665debd08a3b684ae3380e69a4aa99b2bde1: Of course, you'd be able to attack considerably weaker people too, so it can be worth the risk.
textByHashes.4f319fec80df9f2de16cd3e7c4107e7da3b5e502: If you dare to go to the far north-west of the Wilderness, there's a building called the Mage Arena where you can learn to summon the power of Saradomin himself!
textByHashes.94d016a73cc605ed0602021309aa10c737676861: If it's jungle you want, look no further than the southern regions of Karamja.
textByHashes.b838f6a4f4137f5f053b7188b6ee695bc697fd99: Once you get south of Brimhaven, the whole island is pretty much covered in exotic trees, creepers and shrubs.
textByHashes.f854c12033d715c994d4177617f8e79f3e87c16f: There's a small settlement called Tai Bwo Wannai Village in the middle of the island. It's a funny place; the chieftain's an unfriendly chap and his sons are barking mad.
textByHashes.a17d1ae1255c3af620658659eb884961d732f29b: Honestly, one of them asked me to stuff a dead monkey with seaweed so he could EAT it!
textByHashes.f3670a16813487fec3648decdf9619d537e14fc5: Further south you'll find Shilo Village. It's been under attack by terrifying zombies in recent months, if my sources are correct.
textByHashes.70ef20ea80bb1a95f336216888ce6a9bf96c1aac: The jungle's filled with nasty creatures. There are vicious spiders that you can hardly see before they try to bite your legs off, and great big jungle ogres.
textByHashes.be23b1a01eb2b8fc809eb32012b89026443b1dee: Yes, there was a time, back in the Fourth Age, when we humans wouldn't have been able to venture underground. That was before we had magic; the dwarves were quite a threat.
textByHashes.38e1344b95e2eeeb6e83db6f362887f30d7dc727: Still, it's much more friendly now. You can visit the vast dwarven mine if you like; the entrance is on the mountain north of Falador.
textByHashes.b429a57319b87a86955fdf20fd2e9cb3f3f999d2: If you go further west you may be able to visit the dwarven city of Keldagrim. But they were a bit cautious about letting humans in, last time I asked.
textByHashes.7d0da3e926e4d00079bb17bce251e9e6bc440810: On the other hand, if you go west of Brimhaven, you'll find a huge underground labyrinth full of giants, demons, dogs and dragons to fight. It's even bigger than the caves under Taverley, although the Taverley
textByHashes.21dbba9801da45001c6628b6ed8d224fccb0f939: dungeon's pretty good for training your combat skills.
textByHashes.bd1ae185f272ba5c137c06978eb0bea08450497e: Well, you've been to Zanaris. I met a fairy years ago who said she lived there. She was an incredible warrior, considering that she wasn't even a metre tall.
textByHashes.6ec5658e07304c2d9594662ac2dfe38b3b45e4a8: Also, in my research I came across ancient references to some kind of Abyss. Demons from the Abyss have already escaped into this land; Saradomin be thanked that they are very rare!
textByHashes.d2086c0d5125df13c8079ff56d96239c4942f8a9: Ah, Uzer. A city much favoured by Saradomin, destroyed by Thammaron, the elder-demon, late in the Third Age.
textByHashes.38aeca098343f1583b1b840c6d04fdd9fb031cb3: There's a mighty fire-breathing dragon living underground in the deep Wilderness, known as the King Black Dragon. It's a fearsome beast, with a breath that can poison you, freeze you to the ground or
textByHashes.4ee6388c411db210b9952a1141a7ca5d7d126974: But even more deadly is the Queen of the Kalphites. As if her giant mandibles of death were not enough, she also throws her spines at her foes with deadly force. She can even cast rudimentary spells.
textByHashes.1f767b0c4dfc66b1a83a26e869898e1392611fc2: Some dark power must be protecting her, for she can block attacks using prayer just as humans do.
textByHashes.4de62ddddf367ce48de9a755301dac4e4b66a1ac: Another beast that's worthy of a special mention is the Shaikahan. It dwells in the eastern reaches of Karamja, and is almost impossible to kill except with specially prepared weapons.
textByHashes.468432798e6d52f9c4d20a8907a6f4db49319d3d: Many monsters use poison against their foes. If you get poisoned, you will not feel it at the time, but later you will begin to suffer its effects, and your life will drain slowly from you.
textByHashes.f9ca3c445522fa525c0a28df32b8a3b9a230748d: Over time the effects dwindle to nothing, but if you had already been wounded you might die before they wear off completely.
textByHashes.94dee9f8364940aabc2038944e0440a933358a6b: Fortunately, followers of Guthix have devised potions that can cure the poison or even give immunity to it.
textByHashes.0c34f19cc5c3be8db51024ef96501c3600d15e6a: Should you wish to use poison against your own enemies and those of our Lord Saradomin, there is a potion that you can smear on your daggers, arrows, spears, javelins and throwing knives.
textByHashes.815749014efb6fb6d84936a2fdfab0841b313bed: As a general rule, tougher monsters drop more valuable items. But even a lowly hobgoblin can drop valuable gems; it just does this extremely rarely.
textByHashes.f1f90e75c4ac0449516b8dea9955ec09ae7bb2e8: If you can persuade the Slayer Masters to train you as a Slayer, you will be able to fight certain monsters that drop valuable items far more often.
textByHashes.90607171014349a46e0d9d067808d2a9aee65b58: You might care to invest in an enchanted dragonstone ring. These are said to make a monster drop its most valuable items a little more often.
textByHashes.cc4d397541d9946ecf19267e1522c9764c26be6b: Ah, I imagine you see a lot of those.
textByHashes.2110f92474480d0781f566aad32cce047fe605c9: Creatures such as the rock golem, river troll and tree spirit dwell in places where adventurers frequently go to train their skills. While you're training you will often disturb one by accident. It will then get angry and
textByHashes.c33c9430c85a530c5c9c7120bbf6b8af5f0c0b7e: attack you. The safest way to deal with them is to run away immediately, but they sometimes drop valuable items if you kill them.
textByHashes.78678f7653ec3274fc72de836243b7039f43cb44: Ha ha ha... There are plenty of heroes. Always have been, always will be, until the fall of the world.
textByHashes.085ee15c91f1fa48e037fcf2ce629a9739b0d2b4: If you'd do a few more quests, you'd soon become a fairly noted adventurer yourself.
textByHashes.504b6ffda96bfd1cef501c8662a1de6e8c288564: You're quite a noted adventurer yourself!
textByHashes.e151e51ff6a54ad5279ef4ed3a5f57f5147cfb3c: But I suppose I could tell you of a couple...
textByHashes.aeea72d0b9c15113a16fc4b3ac02a0fadeab20f2: Yes, there was a man called Arrav. No-one knew where he came from, but he was a fearsome fighter, a skillful hunter and a remarkable farmer. He lived in the ancient settlement of Avarrocka, defending it from
textByHashes.d8438f2194b62a123a12fe55aa789338b6fea3f7: goblins, until he went forth in search of some strange artefact long desired by the dreaded Mahjarrat.
textByHashes.3b43dcc4aeaecaddfd123de63eca50f0a04250ab: Perhaps some day I shall be able to tell you what became of him.
textByHashes.06ac4e2c5780e197ff8cc80f5f8ba72cc50c25e4: But do not let your head be turned by heroics. Randas was another great man, but he let himself be beguiled into turning to serve Zamorak, and they say he is now a mindless creature deep in the Underground Pass that
textByHashes.3b0d088e392b010d12591f6a6ef2b4709af09d61: Ah, that was quite a discovery! It revolutionised our way of life and jolted us into this Fifth Age of the world.
textByHashes.761a42049e52ea3fa78d07c697f37747f3390445: They say a traveller in the north discovered the key, although no records state exactly what he found. From this he was able to summon the magic of the four elements, using magic as a tool and a weapon. He and
textByHashes.670feba2262d55da912914c75edd2ae93da6ff4e: his followers learnt how to bind the power into little stones so that others could use it.
textByHashes.24dec7e4254737d8f032ad3ba14543964aa5f7eb: in the land south of here they constructed an immense tower where the power could be studied, but followers of Zamorak destroyed it with fire many years ago, and most of the knowledge was lost.
textByHashes.88d4d15a24dda4a7e2b08f8bb88ae4af5d91166e: Perhaps one day those lost secrets will be uncovered once more.
textByHashes.a809b6d559f8a58e3a0a83434e7be0235226c142: I hear you've been very helpful to the wizards who are working on the mysteries of the rune stones.
textByHashes.b3bb826a47d2f51d4d299ffc5d2da540be2ee811: Yes, there are fairly good records of the formation of the cities from primitive settlements.
textByHashes.e3b5c3d31a22941b72b8737633d0d127701b0bfc: In the early part of the Fourth Age, of course, there were no permanent settlements. Tribes wandered the lands, staying where they could until the resources were exhausted.
textByHashes.c05e5ae33e03be23fe25f57fc9493631274297af: This changed as people learnt to grow crops and breed animals, and now there are very few of the old nomadic tribes. There's at least one tribe roaming between the Troll Stronghold and Rellekka, though.
textByHashes.56902365c51a1694f044769afd6422a334efc52d: Truly, their faith in natural spirits is rather foolish, but they're a pleasant enough people.
textByHashes.dd0b912f5d1971caf17a84fa7e17588b9e3d094e: Anyway, what was I saying? Ah, yes...
textByHashes.9e933be484171151ccbcdcccb418b9b0897632e1: One settlement was Avarrocka, a popular trading centre.
textByHashes.f68ea5a356011b4881d81c456c13f78884bb47f4: In the west, Ardougne gradually formed under the leadership of the Carnillean family, despite the threat of the Mahjarrat warlord Hazeel who dwelt in that area until his downfall.
textByHashes.f55a43b53694f7694ea794237580386721597451: Oh dear, I hope you haven't done anything rash!
textByHashes.02fb6860cabfe1b281dd83984490cc6b9e320d4a: So I'd heard. Lord Saradomin is smiling on you.
textByHashes.e800fbc8d9bb185c47b41338efcbac7d3bd088ad: Ah, so you want to know about me, eh?
textByHashes.f6bdbce09c64f25932a6bfc8d95f32a8462eeda7: Mmm... what could I say about myself? Let's see what I've done...
textByHashes.58a0ca4e3b4bdeb39ac96116f4d0892caf7636ea: I've delved into the dungeon west of Brimhaven and heard the terrifying CRASH of the steel dragons battling each other for territory.
textByHashes.d59d42d88e142cd73e55c27361c3d18fd6b6c615: I spent some years on Entrana, where I learnt the techniques of pure meditation.
textByHashes.2a67f3a69eecc3d9f25d8e2a7d87a722493dc469: I've wandered through the vast desert that lies south of Al Kharid and seen the great walls of Menaphos and Sophanem.
textByHashes.7901d75ceef1c59932eae305aceacf920209e7c1: I've always liked to collect little trinkets to remind me of the time when my life was more - ah - exciting.
textByHashes.e558e639ad03e22b56e225e67457ef84f14fd528: Spoken to a sphinx, you say? My dear young lady, you can't possibly be serious!
textByHashes.8ddc353baffdb97c590cbea247ba8c13d1b62f06: Ha ha ha! The people who live deep in the shifting sands of the desert will often tell travellers tall tales of strange beasts, but that doesn't mean any of it's remotely true!
textByHashes.ee5b8c6bc1db5d9a56c36334d6b0d09cb23b69ab: Please, adventurer, there is nothing to be gained by interfering in matters you do not understand.
textByHashes.dba10c1f4f2385abb7df341389c22fc0d7cb4faf: Even if such a creature were to exist...
textByHashes.054dc8b3de9ca558c9c524db81013f70532522c6: EVEN IF SUCH A CREATURE WERE TO EXIST it would be terribly dangerous for you to get involved in its business.
textByHashes.b01fb4b921b0c7dc2b4c1af005bea698f8b3c633: I have heard of terrible things happening to travellers in that land, and I would not wish for you to be dragged into anything like that.
textByHashes.1fda6070891a823f72a52dd3c204d9e1aa98eda7: Indeed, I have heard of adventurers doing the most shameful and blasphemous deeds believing themselves to be acting on behalf of some false god!
textByHashes.8c7a8fbbf991818debc30d86609317bffd9fbc6e: So please do not speak to me of this matter again. Now, I believe I was telling you of my adventurous youth...
textByHashes.f3931cac0d85042232177b45c006f97b3e0848e0: Apart from all that, I've spent many a happy hour in dusty libraries, searching through ancient scrolls and texts for the wisdom of those who have passed on.
textByHashes.15f38cc4e7ecc75e50b54e7dc1641a3fa1781088: Plus plenty of other adventures, quests, journeys... Is there anything else you'd like to know?
textByHashes.75bcfc7c30d018250a86ba7f634eccb981c21d8f: Indeed. This is correct: Saradomin, Guthix and Zamorak.
textByHashes.4e7ae211ddc9028e27040c26136b33c03b849efd: Saradomin, the great and glorious, gives life to this world.
textByHashes.0093601a1e6f2503956901379ebf0ff3442e58ee: Zamorak craves only death and destruction.
textByHashes.61c251edb5ccfc1b1ec96d9c0a117ce71a6e55da: Guthix, calling itself a god of 'balance', holds no allegiance, but simply aids whatever cause suits its shifting purpose.
textByHashes.64bd7c8d9967398fc5250056d417c1613a77f9eb: Please, do not speak further of this. No good can come of meddling in such things.
textByHashes.7e71e53653affd4eaf1d889045a0c4e9fc087678: Adventurer, my patience is limited. Speak to me no further of this - this - this foolishness.
textByHashes.5242ade6975b971379f95dc17d74ca8c7c0f6488: ENOUGH! Begone from me, you foul trickster!
textByHashes.b8b9d578d21ad2649770d398e3631ba3a1f51653: What is this of which you speak?
textByHashes.8eed59a918abdda8f87171bc9c9f53334a6bcb9f: And what makes you believe in this 'Armadyl'?
textByHashes.b9fe616e145a5ad5e620c1b531d312fc96fd7158: So you trusted the word of these people who had been sitting down a hole all their lives?
textByHashes.9253d6c520b62ae35851f7acef896e6392429c3e: I wouldn't worry about this sacred 'armadyllo' anymore. Just another foolish myth.
textByHashes.68087e1208982cd3557fd034a41fc5e34fd1e8e4: That is an impressive feat, adventurer. Truly you must be beloved in the eyes of Saradomin.
textByHashes.cc2ff939d15598421fdd8a7d0c47ea590fb6fd0f: Adventurer, I beg of you, do not meddle in the gods' affairs. No good can come of it.
textByHashes.fe705021ee932902c7b927dc882d7d2ced29387c: Adventurer, what folly leads you to think any of these - things, exist?
textByHashes.7b4dcba48168288fc653464f176e3bb0e985a1a3: It is hot in that land, and the heat can affect a traveller in strange ways. Tell me, when you met 'Icthlarin', had you been feeling at all unwell?
textByHashes.e09f1f15074ca93bd58f2dbe0c8b92d7fd05ee0b: So a strange woman interfered with your mind, then you met a man with the head of a jackal, and you decided he must be a god?
textByHashes.fc11f82a52a6771264ac8ab81c6eb0e9ee1b8a9b: Well, make sure you don't get hypnotised again, and I'm sure you'll not meet any more jackal-headed men claiming to be gods!
textByHashes.2188a9551cf696e84bacf2e370455bba17a5c97e: It'd probably be best if you don't go there again.
textByHashes.4bd05e9887fe208a0683447add9b9935322248fc: And knowing what I know, I do not speak of this matter. Perhaps you would be wise to heed my example.
textByHashes.2f41d5d65f5c9d18ee38befb04637397b91a71fc: Ah, that was a terrible time. The armies of Saradomin fought gloriously against the minions of Zamorak, but many brave warriors and noble cities were overthrown and destroyed utterly.
textByHashes.7554515f60b310cecbea042da1184e6bf5f97659: You have visited Uzer, and seen for yourself the damage that could be wrought by an elder-demon in its wrath.
textByHashes.db0cb9dd4b7580374520485d10e30b9359f520b6: Before the Zamorakian forces could be utterly routed, Lord Saradomin took pity on them and the battle- scarred world, and allowed a truce.
textByHashes.402b011e031166fc285842722710baa671b109eb: Very little is written about the tribe of the Mahjarrat. They are believed to be from the realm of Freneskae, or Frenaskrae - the spelling in this tongue is only approximate.
textByHashes.948e91301fa5d0023c76501423e314b6cf97a18a: One of them, the foul Zamorak, has achieved godhood, although none knows how this came about.
textByHashes.801c5e0b8f18130c8746b5847b063ef83b33899b: Other Mahjarrat who have been particularly active upon this plane are Hazeel, Lucien, Azzanadra and Zemouregal.
textByHashes.08ff73781ae171939163815c5e7c30965b64014e: Adventurer, your actions fill me with concern!
textByHashes.9abc065a2e86abac6d1c07292a9b98048e42c27e: Truly, your bravery exceeds your stature. But I fear his defeat will not be permanent.
textByHashes.ab4824ad40f1b5c1ed3da2b9427ef4def11b4da8: Traveller, I beg of you, do not have further dealings with such creatures! You do not know their power as I do.
textByHashes.c0aa1cfeb4350bd80e6de12d2ab326ff3e3b8bd3: Leave me, traveller; I am old, and your words fill me with horror.
textByHashes.8eb696954547354f4adfebd0f189bb9aaeb12097: I see you are already learning to summon the power of Saradomin.
textByHashes.67a26657fe92adf04ceb6c76192b2e8c07d4ae70: If you travel to the Mage Arena in the north-west reaches of the Wilderness, the battle mage Kolodion may be willing to let you learn to summon the power of Saradomin, should you be able to pass his test.
textByHashes.d9e6192033688a3b22944335b5b5fea50d8b71fd: Why, thank you! I rather like it myself.
textByHashes.6b0b0626ea4238e0159d3911211f4424754af61c: Me? Steal from the bank? Whatever do you mean?
textByHashes.9b2a86a0e26820e9e459cfa7e67b5a495beca464: Oh my goodness, what in the world could make you think I did such a thing?
textByHashes.8f977714ae1262b1d7a18bc7a1ea8d6f55614ac3: So what do you think I did? March across the street, smash the bank wall, knock out the bankers and steal all the money?
textByHashes.cbee6839d75aa5ddaefede25c289129c58d6f4f7: I'm an old man! I walk with a stick! How could I possibly have done that?
textByHashes.48c7b5e23ead54a717126bbd4f2f608f07e465d7: Just run along now, be a good lad, and don't worry yourself about me or my finances!
textByHashes.8b966ea86dd1e0443e79adc007fc8dfd699331b0: Now you've got that off your chest, would you like to ask me about anything else?
textByHashes.32d62c51b8c5a3094aaa5a4746b53dd86e07569a: Stole it? How could you possibly think I did such a thing?
textByHashes.baf4dbbd5f4c87d0ae33453708ee0a7dd3d6a817: Deary me, , your imagination is running wild! What could make you think you saw me do that?
textByHashes.581b3d935b3029f40dcd3cb1d02cb20217e81380: You've seen the bank's security recording?
textByHashes.d6acd673fce70448ad0491a24f01ad0c3cefd13d: Tut tut tut... Oh well, at least you'll never be able to tell the bank about me. They'll never listen.
textByHashes.281a57deec4a2614a0edc560f47aad9ea3077f28: Wrong? WRONG? I'll tell you what's wrong!
textByHashes.a0078806e1016d83f367f4e4d5a04c7501687253: I've spent my whole life travelling the world, doing quests for people, saving lives, saving villages from terrifying monsters and all that sort of thing.
textByHashes.d73ae2f2b00caca00a22b3182e9139a2f5007e05: Now I'm old, and where do I have to live? In this freezing old house next to a pig-sty, with a bunch of yobs outside who can't keep their hands off the market stalls! What sort of reward is that?
textByHashes.2e1d451452a7ea890208c16e73c7fdccb45faa1a: So don't talk to me about right and wrong!
textByHashes.ecb2023f53e66369f981dbd04f1eb0dd8010b615: You could buy one off another player, or wait until they're next made available by the Council.
textByHashes.a6ae5d5b998f0945a8c149bab156598b3cb50212: Ohhh no, I don't intend to part with this. Would you like to ask me about something else?
textByHashes.201a85475aa462e5cd4e570bad130db23c2bd190: No, I think I'll keep it.
textByHashes.fcbaf4b746f65fddf09c39ac907a41e1013ef486: Oh yes, it was certainly a fitting end to my adventuring days. Your fight against the Sea Troll Queen was highly impressive!
textByHashes.ae149b28bb3b3d8024b82dd14b86f3ffe0520953: No, I feel I am a bit old for that kind of thing now. New times need new heroes, and I'm sure you'll always be there to answer the call!
textByHashes.d57d9c6c658483e8bb9373a3fdef004b37af7289: Yes, that's right. I'm an old man, there's no denying it, and I need a nice quiet retirement.
textByHashes.8af1423ddbdcf05d00d2370fb2884d930f1fe8b1: Besides, I think you've proved that the new generation has its own heroes and adventurers - I should step back and let you get on with it!
textByHashes.aca67b163f1e53e4d27d916d18718d25979a3b4e: Well, since you saved my life when we were fighting the Sea Troll Queen, I don't mind telling you... I trust you won't tell anyone about this?
textByHashes.e8eed76ba891d270b805c18e491c6129bcf246b4: I might just have something in mind. After all, that little bank incident went very smoothly.
textByHashes.d922e5014448e6d2124c02854e8c7a83c2c5fc5a: I retired from adventuring and questing. The episode in the bank was hardly a quest, more like a shopping trip!
textByHashes.6d20d0d472cbb72e787ca6086027aae697b15502: I've saved countless villages from unspeakable terrors and slain innumerable evil monsters - surely I deserve some sort of reward in return? Yet what did I get? NOTHING!
textByHashes.ad4e57575aa2147ded8594df9b1c27c87fbb188f: You yourself would get pretty angry if you did a long and dangerous quest and didn't get any reward from it, eh? I'm sure you would!
textByHashes.dbb25fd3b1090362233c72db35e423283557f81e: Well imagine how I felt at the end of a long life of questing when was left penniless, and I had to move into this thief-infested village! This little shack was falling apart when I arrived!
textByHashes.02802b9b13ab34ec763257fa1e891496e8687b66: You go speak to that hag who keeps looking through my window - she'll show you how it used to look before I could afford to redecorate it!
textByHashes.b4b9905fef7c62cc0f5a4a1ea05a72fba5fc337c: Being noble and virtuous simply doesn't put food on the table, and I don't intend to sit in banks begging like so many people do these days.
textByHashes.ea397091839fa2dfdf75786cae3b4b3a4ad464ae: No, it wasn't always like that. I used to be satisfied just knowing that I'd helped, and that was reward enough.
textByHashes.d0195b921f1cb66809fa009d2b1c9077b7130982: But eventually I realised that people forgot about me as soon as the danger was gone, and I wasn't even getting paid properly for my services. Sometimes they offered training in the various skills, but that's no use to me!
textByHashes.2ea53ce280f813443679afe5395ba952eeef465b: I eventually realised that the only way I could get a proper reward for my life's efforts is if I took the reward myself. So I took up my cape and staff and headed for the bank.
textByHashes.1bd98a3a1cebee6a20f3d4f743929d8e797fd18f: Perhaps not... But enough of this matter! Is there anything else you'd like to ask?
textByHashes.c3023ce275dbca6168aa1b010d3c44622f61c788: Humph! You could at least say hello before waving your items in my face.
textByHashes.ee531043e637a4758c677ab8d0575b21076c9c30: I suppose you could use that in combat.
textByHashes.75f4b00b5c6d7cdded12768d550a2a5d33cfe5c3: I suppose you could use that for defense.
textByHashes.b8b15f36f13c62dc57f1a3e763ce22914d4e9c24: Runes are very useful things, but I'll take some of those off your hands for you.
textByHashes.6aba607c8499eeb1376f8a7f6a64541689101296: Ooh, runes? Yes, they're quite valuable.
textByHashes.608f66ceb388677bed748bb45ddd8e8c08a5a8d6: That's called 'money'. Money is fantastically important if you haven't got enough of it, but when you've got as much as I have, you realise that money doesn't matter.
textByHashes.ff14a8aa4369e2f5836b8a9d37abc3a014baf1f8: That's mine. I'll take it back, thank you very much!
textByHashes.02fc0d45af3ab5acbc4f0e310121025fd6f98d43: How did you get your hands on that?!
textByHashes.ed72046bf2450934f6facc76e184160b00b666d5: Get out of here this instant.
textByHashes.6b3fb20af8f06a63cf58af5177df033c7d4b7c83: Tell you what, rather than you showing me items individually, I'll look through all your possessions to see if I can find anything worth discarding.
textByHashes.9fd1005db7e3d9e09f8ada2a0f4ffe3542d2d404: It'd be politer to come and talk to me before you start messing around with my possessions!
textByHashes.4dc262e4a1d9cdd88953192c0660cd84e4104a2d: Oh, do I? Well, why does that interest you?
textByHashes.fd65af5f4994a486f374908e64e0f93c0848e950: Odd? There's nothing odd about a fascination with the magical arts. Besides, the architecture of that Tower is truly remarkable, for one who studies such things.
textByHashes.8f31feef99afeb830bfb90ff7c0f58e3295ec7d0: No, I wouldn't dream of doing anything of the sort!
textByHashes.1596fddbc371e84effe7f51b3f79481bfdfb0ada: No, no. I'm not planning anything like that again.
textByHashes.8892ddabebffa379145d71dec1697f4b660283ca: Well, this is rather embarrassing, but I think there's some kind of monster in my house. Could you go upstairs and get rid of it, please?
textByHashes.809e6a103507279a22a1b6f24aad01e4e4541d2d: I think it's somewhere upstairs. It kept me awake all last night.
textByHashes.dba153e41ace3f507f5d100a544d631f4cfe58d6: I need some bronze bars. Please bring me .
textByHashes.b0c492b1d3c1ab0a879e6df1646b5f8c75d45210: Mine some copper ore and tin ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of bronze.
textByHashes.3e462d435f1eed05f670e3631d65bab87f5a5acc: I need a few bronze daggers to keep the goblins at bay. Please bring me .
textByHashes.3d7be889f09f469da37f1e21be88848d3fc871ef: I'd like some maces made of bronze. Please bring me .
textByHashes.c827e138b7935b657e6830fb3daf4b7944cc9d7a: I need a few bronze arrowheads to complete some arrows I was making. Please bring me .
textByHashes.0d109db54083fa218a3c76a19d47d7f7eee44204: I'd like some bronze knives to throw at goblins. Please bring me .
textByHashes.5713d615d9e0f925377173ebb705303af9b5d321: My weapons collection isn't all it used to be. Some short swords made of bronze would be much appreciated. Please bring me .
textByHashes.80b56d6021972b9f0eb1b6fec2c116b6173bdabc: The head fell off my axe when I was chopping wood last week, so I need some more bronze axes. Please bring me .
textByHashes.97a59af4d1c069634522d098cbbd20858e415b9c: Could you fetch me some big warhammers made of bronze? Please bring me .
textByHashes.0ccbbf7e9ea16a597694b07e05b0a53ca608ea52: I could do with some medium-sized helmets. I'd like bronze ones. Please bring me .
textByHashes.893c5a62f3e2c27937c582bf7d381d632845cb70: I need some lengths of bronze wire to repair something. Please bring me .
textByHashes.c47ebf17e5fea690c1cc9f4040d81b1b8930adf1: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make daggers.
textByHashes.d5524036c392077a160290e69332d257e4a7c556: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make maces.
textByHashes.77f76d83cd9e490b10468b92ae47e5efe7377a92: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make arrowheads.
textByHashes.7a72c2ee2875f387cf9c8a424da60b95447ebc4e: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make throwing knives.
textByHashes.4d0a41d5958559023235a5c1e78c2271209d54d9: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make short swords.
textByHashes.363bdd3446a8fd8f255f45f00669d5a91c25df97: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make axes.
textByHashes.089f23809eca19ecbfd32898b2a8ba682800fbfe: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make warhammers.
textByHashes.1371cdfa6946bc642421521f798389d96d6b8c00: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make helmets.
textByHashes.f4956a1b1525b5e864ed9d9eda48bb63430b494e: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make wire.
textByHashes.2eed6195d0b8d3129dbb94682bea3375c21228fc: I need some iron bars. Please bring me .
textByHashes.e8cc90764bef17a41c3a2040797dbfa01474a65e: Mine some iron ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of iron.
textByHashes.75e8414f136cdce1200e4d8e8c228b4d6af581a1: I need some iron arrowheads to put on the arrows I was making. Please bring me .
textByHashes.99c80f060a71fb220aa03c16c0445d6912c32840: I could do with some iron throwing knives to keep the goblins away from my house. Please bring me .
textByHashes.90e7864331012dea1f1665744eaa198532c641e6: I'd like some chunky iron warhammers. Please bring me .
textByHashes.17425b091e7696f0f565027c072c24a3b494dfaf: Some iron maces would be useful. Please bring me .
textByHashes.ef0cc99e87e51f4363b0704423a74c105749e590: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make arrowtips.
textByHashes.3d80e4666a9769c40674321ce9b8ce56129286c1: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make throwing knives.
textByHashes.baf03622940ff97c1fe801af65ccffa526055125: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make warhammers.
textByHashes.3a8de0c9c1a51f4b2fd06308e931aac37270f823: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make maces.
textByHashes.433a24a4bbf8aa7c098a87e173d2743405b0c49b: A few lumps of iron ore would be nice. Please bring me .
textByHashes.92e24cc0b879279918cb6e41076e53ca9d730953: It should be easy enough for you to mine some iron ore.
textByHashes.3d3c9fabfebb30c38774d4790615acb2741bbccc: I've found a use for cadava berries. Please bring me .
textByHashes.d67e1cd14ea1c8fc5e4c2e25b4d9ac36cde9d8af: Just look around the woods south-west of Varrock.
textByHashes.3aca002a6bcb4629c4bcef75c7f7cb47d06b712c: The hail can be very heavy here, so I'd like a few leather cowls. Please bring me .
textByHashes.f0eb5e1525858fa8c082feda2c7a259c324e9db0: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the cowls yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.
textByHashes.32475b592c461bacc4e78f441c917e8306275d80: Strange as it may seem, I want a lot of beer! Please bring me .
textByHashes.3a3d8d57280eea72159b6e5e2ba078251ceee55d: There's a pub not far from here that will sell you plenty of that. Go west to Port Sarim.
textByHashes.baefdb466cd5b3f977b6b8d4c7aaf8bd2d8f0cd2: I don't have a decent larder here, so I can't store food very well. Now I'm out of loaves of bread. Please bring me .
textByHashes.5cef3a5b66d909358319715fcfc1ab824eb8c8a7: Mix some flour and water to make bread dough, then bake it on a cooking range.
textByHashes.5757dc3e70c0466490f035e9c62b3df993b16d48: I'll be needing a few pieces of soft leather. Please bring me .
textByHashes.6e5ff54eb31d94b689dd06d9b6b0b9f22887a04e: There's a tanner in Al Kharid who will turn cowhides into leather.
textByHashes.59fa2842e807c443eb4bf0f906c5d3eab7f0ecaf: I saw an interesting bed in Karamja called a 'hammock'. It seemed to be made out of string, and I'd like some balls of wool so I can make my own. Please bring me .
textByHashes.5e3dd65b597e6d7a7cc86431abee8f3a12f1cdae: You can buy shears from the general store east of here. Shear some sheep, then spin the wool into balls on the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle.
textByHashes.57613bae7b1a7a5fe796c8aa6ca7166cc6736b61: It's a bit nippy in this house, and my hands are cold. I need some leather gloves. Please bring me .
textByHashes.37dfb0f2ecb4567ed96f4e4a5a43865706a63238: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to sew the gloves yourself. Buy a needle & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid.
textByHashes.525998867a3a59456c96f6b7fe82412066e4756e: I'm going to make an omelette, so I'll need some eggs. Please bring me .
textByHashes.d7a7df16c7dcd6b21acb5d6d751b5accf442bbfe: Eggs are usually found where chickens are farmed.
textByHashes.436d2221429cd157428da62c10442bc3888ff586: My undergarments are getting a bit worn out. I'll be needing some sheets of silk to patch them. Please bring me .
textByHashes.3a8b540d4f8e8087e93839fb21367b603d04934d: There's a man in Al Kharid who sells it very cheaply. If you ever take any to Ardougne you'll get a good profit.
textByHashes.251b60cf736e07f6a3c4769aa973eea93c5f314b: I'll need some clay that's been softened so I can craft it. Please bring me .
textByHashes.b80dee8c1d2159acbe7efc4992b8e8ccb33bc8c1: If you mine some clay, you can use containers of water on it to soften it.
textByHashes.d895e7e723b9c47547010bceadaa23f5b39a7d8a: Believe it or not, I need a few unfired clay pots. Please bring me .
textByHashes.c4b709a8bbd3814b2ae865d31909ee020b938711: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pots. Just don't bake them.
textByHashes.075599899d52b9bc6e670b36e0401237a93dacaf: My roof is leaking, so I need some swamp paste to fix it. Please bring me .
textByHashes.6a3be1d689b4ae47d82e87a4c93ce5572246420c: Swamp tar is found south-east of here. Add it to a pot of flour and cook it on an open fire to make the paste.
textByHashes.bbb3831cab44cdfd5da2c9558013262ad79c9215: I need some potatoes, if you'd be so kind. Please bring me .
textByHashes.c2412aad5ceb1a61bc0b9709a5e5e1005b65e76b: There's a field of those north of Lumbridge.
textByHashes.52163de837c8b8e510ffd591c4e3d0d7f2e52ba5: I want to make some arrows, so I'll need some headless arrows that have the feathers attached. Please bring me .
textByHashes.bd44a2147f027082a43d9fd28a2b561abb305659: Use a knife to chop some logs into arrowshafts. Then add a feather to each arrowshaft.
textByHashes.cf46241d4e79e4e5f95d529986e1e7192ca82e35: I need some bronze spears. Please bring me .
textByHashes.8f7516963c840709eaa34c9185935e53598abf22: If you kill some of those pesky goblins, you're bound to get some bronze spears.
textByHashes.e868029505b1ba63c1043b53781ea696196fa17c: My glassware got damaged when I moved here, so I need some new beer glasses. Please bring me .
textByHashes.6bad38e70af3ef86ebe55300d93a33f2e668d577: If you get some seaweed and a bucket of sand, you'll be able to mix them at a furnace to make molten glass. Then use a glassblowing pipe to make beer glasses. Most of what you need can be found on Entrana.
textByHashes.dc73bc567c55a4da93e6e1d58e106a359430d711: My plans for today require some sets of bones, the normal-sized sort you get from goblins. Please bring me .
textByHashes.6516ca811025cb6b32769b301d88cb7171c4836d: You'll find bones easily enough if you fight creatures.
textByHashes.29d1ea92f8a93d6180847eaa13a38e76f6aadc4c: Strange as this may seem, I need some unfired pie dishes. Please bring me .
textByHashes.2c445554e956f1667dd8fa9cba3c90947784f0a3: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pie dishes. Just don't bake them.
textByHashes.6eb807f5f77fae116bc4fbfd2853ab7f8f573d84: I hear pet cats are getting popular these days. I'd like some raw rat in case I get one. Please bring me .
textByHashes.9740cb874acb1b17d51852dc4f04ac4f4d64d867: You should find some big rats south-east of here in the swamp.
textByHashes.a075ab84722b62177ec20062826226d81b2bcb7e: I need a few lumps of copper ore. Please bring me .
textByHashes.f69d12473b2af2c6249911a849c32e339612303b: It should be easy enough for you to mine some copper ore.
textByHashes.3c019f349d4e58570918f1f224b22caa5d20184f: I'm out of flour, so I could do with a few pots of that. Please bring me .
textByHashes.0f9975fc079cc97a97faac641c4148efb85ebce0: There's a windmill north of here. You can use it to grind grain into flour. Then use an empty pot to collect the flour.
textByHashes.34555d49b61761981941b7169573584773bdba7d: I need a handful of feathers to stick in my beard. Please bring me .
textByHashes.ebdc61cc6584cd552603153c37d77b3728313e42: The cheapest way to get feathers is to kill chickens.
textByHashes.81f43d631d6ae824e20d5f235f494d91d18a5c35: This house is a bit cold, so I could do with some normal logs to burn. Please bring me .
textByHashes.e705d3c13888968d858db3c37cd939dd4d633b45: I suggest you take an axe and chop down some standard trees.
textByHashes.fd4464bac5d27ddc23c98a820c1bd5a4c4d36306: I'm running short of food, so I'd like some cooked chickens. Please bring me .
textByHashes.c828096a629d38e6a09b12128e72bdfd257a5b6b: You could try killing some chickens, then cooking their meat.
textByHashes.8507992d2e1250bbbd988482cff86ec2763d90fe: My footwear is getting a bit worn out, so I need a few pairs of leather boots. Please bring me .
textByHashes.97ecea2ad950079af512fd9a9c181baf47416d98: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the boots yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.
textByHashes.0b46a794852eeda5b3950bda2b8bf5dcaa74ec3c: I'd like a few cowhides. Please bring me .
textByHashes.cdfd7cd16d7f97befea75c11f9ea0d8850103147: If you slaughter some cows, you'll get cowhides easily enough.
textByHashes.3ef664957be588674661fcd7ad1a14267b7bb15c: My shortbow's string is getting a bit worn out, so could you fetch me some bow strings? Please bring me .
textByHashes.60b0f8bc0bfca33b782112573771bc0e9399aaab: If you pick some flax, you can use the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle to spin it into bowstrings.
textByHashes.68764974de5d863ba27793634e097d8b607ccbfe: I'm a bit hungry. I'd like some cooked meat, not chicken or any sort of bird. And definitely not camel or rabbit either! Please bring me .
textByHashes.9b8e5c8ad25d79df3bffa02125a455cbcc8fe7a6: Just cook some beef, rat or bear.
textByHashes.2a2f0d3d6d228982fae3735e18e117b457302dd3: I'm short of ammunition, so I could do with some bronze arrows. That way I can shoot at goblins through my window. Please bring me .
textByHashes.3896fc618921b16a2b98cba5bdb2d84453af0978: You can make them by chopping a log into arrowshafts and adding feathers to them, then smithing a bronze bar into arrowheads to complete the arrows.
textByHashes.1190f5b8f4ac111e84441f03f41f5ff1e3277dfa: I'd like a bit of grain. Please bring me .
textByHashes.1e0658a08a029da110b8e9a24a39d16456622005: There's a field full of it north of here.
textByHashes.0dc9d4e652143ca14a53294ce318fbf0edddc47f: I need a few lumps of tin ore. Please bring me .
textByHashes.8398555498c0da17763f0d5f1f159d9a36c387f9: You shouldn't have any trouble mining tin ore.
textByHashes.57a7a5e6bf18f27dfd247efa8696161dc5423b08: I could do with some freshly cooked anchovies for a salad I'm planning. Please bring me .
textByHashes.77085fa542ba0d940e1fe259f2a5d69ee9db17ed: I could do with some freshly cooked shrimps for a salad I'm planning. Please bring me .
textByHashes.409d8e3e67e8a2bf189d27f104a7de533406e0c5: Use a small fishing net to get anchovies from the sea south of here. Then cook them on a fire or range.
textByHashes.eb8494af23f664a6153267c0dba926d331c45394: Use a small fishing net to get shrimps from the sea south of here. Then cook them on a fire or range.
textByHashes.315dafbb1da5418e43561f4f6dbfaac34e5e6823: I'd like to study the rune essence that the wizards have been talking about recently, so I need a few pieces. Please bring me .
textByHashes.e4215835f80b7ff29b3851b0c0bdc13c131a4414: Ask the Archmage in the tower south of here to teleport you to the Rune Essence mine. Then you can mine me some pieces.
textByHashes.dc99816ce40938a60cced9dff995107c98fde54a: Some chunks of molten glass would be the ideal patch for my cracked window. Please bring me .
textByHashes.528c2fbb551bb7d81055a82db75a77f681f4a50e: Use a bucket of sand with some soda ash on a furnace.
textByHashes.480f31fe8e490785d2de7375786c6c9c6d07e6f4: Ah, thank you very much! Now I shall be able to sleep in peace.
textByHashes.185dcc6fb8ef69ce6ef9e9f563357f6e103e088f: Allow me to offer you an appropriate reward for your assistance...
textByHashes.78afeac08e3369775231d500050f37f43c2db9fb: Thank you, thank you! In thanks, I shall bestow on you a simple blessing.
textByHashes.ebd7dc4bc173199bd2c98fcb1ec6b3d2a49c5c71: Thank you, thank you! Please take this money as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.99a28563b03382c775affa28dccd0434b1c41a23: Thank you, thank you! Please take these seeds as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.a76c694843a7e145b46039f88b415923713b5f66: Thank you, thank you! Please take these herbs as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.5023442095c9519ad071b2cda3091ab22e19b6af: Thank you, thank you! Please take these runes as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.1dea94cf9a3523d422358560da3f3666fd289b7e: Thank you, thank you! Please take this gem as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.8e387c34e4134f3dd2110010d55ecaf7f6a3f5d0: Those are banknotes! I can't use those!
textByHashes.f701304a10b3fc155675a4d66ee6277deb4163f7: I'd give you a reward, but you don't seem to have any space for it. Come back when you do.
textByHashes.da5a8a6e24335eedd75123ddf379b3949d3999e8: If you come back with at least one inventory space, I'll give you another copy.
textByHashes.e58407823ff9fe71ce2941404fd4dd722d84b8cf: Father Aereck in Lumbridge is sure to reward you if you take a note to him for me.
textByHashes.53c28c3b49fe9fd52ec580e67a446c5d54ab2cfd: Just head eastwards to Lumbridge. He'll be in the church.
textByHashes.41222693a1d03bf82f2708e2ae1079c3dbf4a033: Could you please take a message to the High Priest of Entrana for me?
textByHashes.0094fe04a6be440963f042663abcd6ed94cd7dc3: There are some monks in Port Sarim, west of here, who will take you there so long as you're not carrying any weapons or armour.
textByHashes.0e60dc7afe8ca22fe89e0e178cfacdfbbd09fb71: Reldo, the librarian in Varrock Palace, wrote to me recently about something. I need you to take my reply to him.
textByHashes.1a1b2002cfdfeb02cee2a54e4dbc49e413d5d4f6: The library is on the ground floor of the Palace in Varrock.
textByHashes.2ff7932668baf9e5e9055756fc2a93ebc2058808: Thurgo the dwarf is from an ancient tribe. I've found some of its history that he was asking about, and I need you to deliver the information to him.
textByHashes.452eb23492bd0488e548b64d481ffbedd6ed0662: Go west to Port Sarim, then southwards to some cliffs. He lives on the beach there.
textByHashes.b9cab929b56d0cbcfd961ed1e2a8999359142c3f: I hear my old friend Fr Lawrence in Varrock is drinking a bit too much. I'd like to get a letter to him about it.
textByHashes.61a0c991857bb8e8adccd5de86d9931c80945a8b: Varrock is a fairly long way north-east of here. The church is at the eastern side of the town, near the Palace.
textByHashes.60e3d302b578ded6ef8146cb6af2b4e0eac59241: I've written a letter to my old friend Abbot Langley that I'd like you to deliver.
textByHashes.f9b99e344b15d9d3c252bf70de2440b570e2fdee: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you find his Monastery. He'll be downstairs.
textByHashes.dbc80749b68d465295d354c6edd2fc572170196d: I'd like to get a note up to the Oracle on Ice Mountain.
textByHashes.12acb1ab7dea9a396347e46b0b2f834c07dbbae8: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you see the Ice Mountain. It'll be at the top.
textByHashes.74153430b69fe3eb5479c8f9f33b390033189aa9: Yes, you're meant to be delivering it for me!
textByHashes.2be3139406beb4547e66fdf5f6acedbaff66d21f: I asked you to deliver that for me. But I may as well take it back now.
textByHashes.d223cbf1c55fb557b31f5e36106a733be543f500: You seem to have mislaid my letter, so here's another copy.
textByHashes.2d27127a382df9a274289420d5353865c38ccefa: I need you to answer this for me.
textByHashes.2ba3fa01f7b19ac0b94e945a7cc6928e5bc24315: Please enter the answer to the question.
textByHashes.41d48f865b9452274643b7a54ca2f2d9d46f50e9: How can that be? Try again!
